content.claim: Since the 2011 Public Education Act and consolidation in 2013, governance authority over schools shifted from approximately 198 municipal bodies to a unified national Educational Authority, centralizing teacher hiring, pay scales, curriculum content, and financial control while principals lost authority over hiring and budgets.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Implementation planning lens
limitations: Describes formal structural change but does not quantify impact on decision-making speed or implementation fidelity
Does not assess school-level stakeholder perception of centralization trade-offs
Baseline municipal autonomy period predates current administrative era
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
